name: CANFIELD RES (TOM & JUDY)
address: 20728 SE IRON HORSE LANE, BEND, OR 97702

fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility and accessibility of entry points to the main building, such as ensuring clearly marked driveways and pedestrian walkways, could facilitate quick responder access. Well-maintained pathways and openly marked routes may assist in efficient navigation throughout the property, allowing emergency personnel to easily identify and access critical areas. Maintaining the landscaped areas so that they do not obstruct driveways and entry points would support unobstructed movement and operational staging. Improved lighting along paths and driveways may enhance visibility for responders during nighttime operations, optimizing the speed and safety of emergency interventions. Ensuring that pedestrian walkways are kept clear and easily navigable can assist in the smooth movement of personnel and equipment to and from the main building.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the structure could be achieved by increasing the separation of vegetation adjacent to the building, which currently limits the defensible space. Managing the scattered shrubs and considering their reduction, especially near the structure, may minimize fuel continuity and possible ignition sources close to improvements. Maintaining and, if feasible, expanding open terrain around the property might further enhance defensible space, reducing the likelihood of wildfire reaching the structure. Consider ongoing management of the vegetated perimeter to ensure it remains open and accessible, potentially providing a buffer and enabling firefighter access and suppression efforts. Proactive vegetation management and maintaining clear, navigable access routes could improve long-term wildfire resilience, supporting both immediate property preparedness and future mitigation efforts.
